## Summary

Describe the controlled task or coherent task group, the intended outcome, and
why it belongs in VyrnForge UI.

## Task identity

- **Task ID(s):**
- **Sprint:**
- **Quality gate:**
- **Workstream:**
- **Accountable owner:**
- **Manifest lifecycle state:** Draft / Implementation / Review / Accepted / Released / Superseded

## Scope and dependencies

**Affected packages, components, files/areas:**

**Explicitly out of scope:**

**Predecessor tasks, required branches/documents, and finish-to-start evidence:**

**Cross-workstream owners, hand-off, and integration target:**

## Classification and compatibility

- [ ] New capability
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Verification or refinement
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Testing
- [ ] Governance
- [ ] Release

| Surface | Impact (`none` / `additive` / `breaking` / `not applicable`) | Explanation or migration link |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Public API / package exports |  |  |
| CSS class or variable |  |  |
| Runtime or peer dependencies |  |  |
| Browser/runtime compatibility |  |  |
| Migration |  |  |

## Quality impact

Record the required or not-applicable rationale for unit/pure-logic, DOM,
browser, accessibility, keyboard, theme/density, performance, and
documentation evidence.

## Validation

- **Commands executed and results:**
- **Test reports:**
- **Screenshots or browser traces when relevant:**
- **API diff, package verification, and consumer fixture proof when relevant:**
- **Known limitations and follow-up tasks:**

## Pull-request controls

- [ ] This PR is one controlled task or a coherent, explicitly listed task group.
- [ ] No opportunistic refactoring or unrelated formatting change is included.
- [ ] Public API changes are documented and have the required review.
- [ ] Predecessor tasks are complete or have an approved waiver.
- [ ] No maturity status is promoted without canonical maturity evidence.
- [ ] This task branch will not merge directly into `main`.
- [ ] S0–S8 work remains improvement/controlled-hardening work until an approved release gate.

## Exception or waiver

If an exception is requested, record its reason, risk and mitigation, approver
role and approval link, expiry or follow-up task, and evidence. No silent
waivers are permitted.
